title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control

yaml.title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
yaml.description: Efficacy assessment of new formulation of Ivermectin : pre-clinical phase.

markdown.markdown:
This project aimed to evaluate new long-acting injectable formulations of ivermectin as a complementary strategy for malaria vector control. A key challenge was extending the duration of efficacy beyond current limitations (<7 days).


## **Objectives**  

- Identify the optimal formulation and dose meeting WHO Preferred Product Characteristics (HR > 4 for at least one month)
- Evaluate efficacy in semi-field conditions (cattle–wild mosquitoes)
- Estimate the epidemiological impact on malaria prevalence
- Assess resistance development in *Anopheles* and *Aedes*
- Study potential behavioural effects on mosquito host-seeking


## **My role** 

- Designed study protocols, including sample size estimation and power calculations

- Developed and implemented the statistical analysis plan

- Performed data management, cleaning, and quality control

- Conducted statistical analyses (survival models, GLMM, GAM, PK/PD, dose-response; epidemiological model)

- Supervised students and supported partners in data analysis and interpretation

- Communicated results to diverse stakeholders (scientific and non-scientific audiences)


## **Methods and tools**  

- Survival analysis, GLMM, GAM, PK/PD modelling, non-linear dose-response, epidemiological model 
SAP, DMP redaction, study design, sample size estimation, power calculation, indicator of efficacy creation
- R (tidyverse, ggplot2, glmmTMB, coxme, mgcv, drc, PKNCA)
- Data management and reporting (R, Excel, Word, Quarto)
- Study design, sample size estimation, power calculation, indicator of efficacy creation

## **Key Results** 

- Sustained mosquitocidal efficacy for at least two months with HR > 4, meeting WHO PPC
- Repeated treatment campaigns significantly reduced malaria prevalence (up to ~78%)
- Evidence of differential resistance between Aedes and Anopheles
- No significant effect on host-seeking behaviour

## **Impact**  
Contributed to the development of a novel malaria control strategy aligned with WHO recommendations
Supported the selection of a lead formulation, enabling progression to Phase I clinical trials
Provided key insights for deployment strategies, including resistance management


## **Publications** 

@sagna_ivermectin_2023 ; @pooda_impact_2026 ; @zela_selection_2026 ; @dongmo_modeling_2025
